title: ICCR to host Dhaka University Hindi scholars on nine-day India visit
summary: Indian Council for Cultural Relations, ICCR will host a distinguished delegation of Hindi scholars comprising students and faculty from Dhaka University’s Institute of Modern Language for a nine-day visit beginning tomorrow. External Affairs Ministry said in...
body: Indian Council for Cultural Relations, ICCR will host a distinguished delegation of Hindi scholars comprising students and faculty from Dhaka University’s Institute of Modern Language for a nine-day visit beginning tomorrow. External Affairs Ministry said in a statement that the tour is a joint initiative of the ICCR and the High Commission of India in Dhaka. It has been organised under the ICCR’s Students Visitors Programme 2026-27. The delegation will engage in a rich schedule of academic and literary interactions at some of India’s foremost institutions.

title: Govt gives one-year extension to Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri till 14th July 2027
summary: The government today gave a one-year extension to Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri till 14th July next year. The Appointments Committee of the Cabinet approved a one-year extension in service for Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri, who is a 1989 batch IFS officer,...
body: The government today gave a one-year extension to Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri till 14th July next year. The Appointments Committee of the Cabinet approved a one-year extension in service for Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri, who is a 1989 batch IFS officer, beyond 14th July this year. Mr Misri assumed charge as Foreign Secretary on 15th July 2024. He was India’s Ambassador to China from January 2019 to December 2021. He has also served as Private Secretary to three Prime Ministers of India – I.K. Gujral, Dr. Manmohan Singh and Narendra Modi.

title: EPFO notifies 8.25% interest rate on EPF deposits for FY 2025-26
summary: Employees’ Provident Fund Organisation, EPFO has officially notified an 8.25 percent interest rate on EPF deposits for financial year 2025-26. The ratings have been declared following approval of the Ministry of Labour and Employment as per the EPF Scheme,...
body: Employees’ Provident Fund Organisation, EPFO has officially notified an 8.25 percent interest rate on EPF deposits for financial year 2025-26. The ratings have been declared following approval of the Ministry of Labour and Employment as per the EPF Scheme, 1952. It has directed Regional and Zonal Offices to credit the approved interest to members’ EPF accounts.

title: Chartered Accountants play an indispensable role in strengthening India’s economy: VP Radhakrishnan
summary: Vice President C P Radhakrishnan today said that Chartered Accountants (CA) play an indispensable role in strengthening India’s economy by upholding integrity, transparency and ethical governance. Addressing the 78th Chartered Accountants’ Day in New Delhi,...
body: Vice President C P Radhakrishnan today said that Chartered Accountants (CA) play an indispensable role in strengthening India’s economy by upholding integrity, transparency and ethical governance. Addressing the 78th Chartered Accountants’ Day in New Delhi, Mr. Radhakrishnan congratulated members and students of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI) and praised the institute for maintaining high professional standards and urged it not to dilute these standards, as it is safeguarding the system. He said that the country’s financial ecosystem depends on competent and ethical professionals. Highlighting the role of CAs in the reforms such as GST, the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, Direct Benefit Transfer and the new Income Tax framework, the Vice President stressed that the profession serves as the custodian of trust in the country’s financial system. Mr Radhakrishnan also called upon Chartered Accountants to support entrepreneurs, strengthen district-level associations to develop practical knowledge of products identified under the One District One Product (ODOP) initiative. He said such expertise would enable them to guide producers in reducing costs without compromising quality, thereby enhancing competitiveness and strengthening local economies. The Vice President also urged ICAI to maintain its rigorous professional and examination standards, saying quality should never be compromised. On the occasion, the Vice-President launched the Peer Review Board (PRB) Web Portal and ICAI Jan Utsav Portal, developed in collaboration with MyGov.

title: SC lays down guidelines on computation of annual income of motor accident victims
summary: The Supreme Court today laid down guidelines on how courts should determine the annual income of deceased victims in motor accident or injured claimants on the basis of Income Tax Returns. In a significant verdict aimed at bringing uniformity to motor...
body: The Supreme Court today laid down guidelines on how courts should determine the annual income of deceased victims in motor accident or injured claimants on the basis of Income Tax Returns. In a significant verdict aimed at bringing uniformity to motor accident compensation, the top court held that for salaried individuals, only the Income Tax Return (ITR) of the previous year will be sufficient for showcasing the annual income. However, where a promotion or salary revision had taken place shortly before the accident and was not fully reflected in the latest return, courts could also consider promotion letters and other corroborative financial records, it said. In case of self-employed persons, it held that the average income disclosed in ITRs for up to the previous three years should ordinarily serve as the reference point. The verdict then referred to scenarios which may lead to fluctuations in income of the deceased or injured self-employed victims and said these would include the nature of the business, growth pattern and impact of death on the business and potential growth of business. It also referred to negative income and any other relevant factor relating to the business that may affect the income of the accident victims. A bench of justices Sanjay Karol and N Kotiswar Singh held that there cannot be a rigid formula for computing annual income under the Motor Vehicles Act, but drew a clear distinction between salaried employees and self-employed persons for the purpose of assessing compensation based on their ITRs. Recognising conflicting approaches adopted by different courts across the country, the bench observed that while some courts relied only on the latest ITR, others averaged income reflected in returns for the preceding years, leading to inconsistent awards.

title: Govt issues notice to Meta regarding rollout of username feature on WhatsApp in India
summary: The government today issued a notice to Meta regarding the rollout of the username feature on WhatsApp in India. Sources said that the Ministry of Information and Technology directed Meta to furnish a detailed explanation on the usernames feature within three...
body: The government today issued a notice to Meta regarding the rollout of the username feature on WhatsApp in India. Sources said that the Ministry of Information and Technology directed Meta to furnish a detailed explanation on the usernames feature within three days. The Ministry also directed Meta not to roll out the usernames feature until consultation on the matter is over. Sources said that the Ministry has taken note of a public announcement by WhatsApp that it has commenced a phased global rollout, including in India, of its Usernames feature. It said that once the feature is enabled, the recipient’s phone number will no longer be visible to a first-time contact and that an optional username key may be set as an additional control. Sources said there are concerns that the feature may increase incidence of online frauds, phishing, digital arrest scams and impersonation attacks. Concerns have also been raised that the feature may facilitate impersonation and identity spoofing.

title: India, EU working to expedite inclusion of Indian ship recycling yards under EU Ship Recycling Regulation: Union Minister Sonowal
summary: Minister for Ports, Shipping and Waterways Sarbananda Sonowal has said that India and the European Union are working to expedite the inclusion of Indian ship recycling yards under the EU Ship Recycling Regulation. In a social media post following his...
body: Minister for Ports, Shipping and Waterways Sarbananda Sonowal has said that India and the European Union are working to expedite the inclusion of Indian ship recycling yards under the EU Ship Recycling Regulation. In a social media post following his conversation with European Commissioner for Environment, Water Resilience and a Competitive Circular Economy, Jessika Roswall, Mr. Sonowal shared that the two sides discussed a roadmap for seamless coordination. He said they also reaffirmed their commitment to sustainable global standards and the broader India-EU Free Trade Agreement goals. Highlighting India’s leadership in green maritime practices, the Minister said the country is currently the world’s largest ship recycling nation. He added that, backed by an eight-billion-dollar financial commitment to shipbuilding and ship recycling, India aims to recycle around 16 thousand ships over the next decade.

title: FAO and WMO release report on extreme heat and agriculture
summary: Food and Agriculture Organisation of the United Nations (FAO) and World Meteorological Organization (WMO) jointly released a report titled “Extreme Heat and Agriculture” yesterday. The report said that rising temperatures and extreme heat are reducing food...
body: Food and Agriculture Organisation of the United Nations (FAO) and World Meteorological Organization (WMO) jointly released a report titled “Extreme Heat and Agriculture” yesterday. The report said that rising temperatures and extreme heat are reducing food production and incomes in agrifood systems that 1.23 billion people rely on. It also warned that agricultural yield losses can triple when heat combines with other hazards such as drought and are projected to get much worse as the world warms. The report further said that extreme heat is emerging as one of the most urgent and least understood threats to agriculture and food security. It said that rising temperatures, prolonged heatwaves, and shifting climate patterns are already disrupting crop yields, livestock health, water availability, and rural livelihoods – with impacts falling disproportionately on the most vulnerable.

title: President Droupadi Murmu to grace first convocation of Central University of Andhra Pradesh today
summary: President Droupadi Murmu will grace the first convocation of the Central University of Andhra Pradesh at Jantuluru in Ananthapuramu district today. The President will confer degrees on 1,268 students who have successfully completed undergraduate and...
body: President Droupadi Murmu will grace the first convocation of the Central University of Andhra Pradesh at Jantuluru in Ananthapuramu district today. The President will confer degrees on 1,268 students who have successfully completed undergraduate and postgraduate programmes. She will also present gold medals to 56 students for their outstanding academic performance. Established in 2018, the Central University of Andhra Pradesh offers 18 postgraduate and nine undergraduate programmes. The university also runs Ph.D. programmes in six departments and 15 diploma courses. The convocation marks a significant milestone in the academic journey of the university since its establishment.

title: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi to arrive in New Delhi this evening on a three-day visit to India
summary: Prime Minister of Japan, Sanae Takaichi, will arrive in New Delhi this evening on a three-day visit to India. This will be the first official visit of Prime Minister Takaichi to India. She will attend the 16th India-Japan Annual Summit tomorrow with Prime...
body: Prime Minister of Japan, Sanae Takaichi, will arrive in New Delhi this evening on a three-day visit to India. This will be the first official visit of Prime Minister Takaichi to India. She will attend the 16th India-Japan Annual Summit tomorrow with Prime Minister Narendra Modi. The Summit will provide an opportunity for both sides to review and strengthen the full spectrum of bilateral cooperation as well as exchange views on regional and global issues of mutual interest. Our correspondent reports that India and Japan share a long-standing friendship rooted in centuries of cultural and civilizational exchange, spiritual affinity, and shared values of freedom, democracy and respect for the rule of law. India-Japan bilateral relationship was elevated to a Special Strategic and Global Partnership in 2014. As the two countries approach the 75th anniversary of the establishment of diplomatic relations in 2027, cooperation continues to deepen across a wide range of sectors. These include trade and investment, economic security, defense and security, science and technology, culture, and people-to-people ties. India-Japan Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement came into effect on 1 August 2011. In 2025-26, bilateral trade crossed 27 billion dollars. Japan is the 5th largest investor in India, with investment of 48.17 billion dollars between April 2000 and March 2026. Cultural cooperation also remains strong, with the Japanese showing great interest in Indian culture, including yoga, and finding connections through Buddhism and cultural symbolism. Suparna Saikia, Akashvani News, Delhi.

title: Venezuela earthquake death toll rises to 1,943
summary: Death toll in Venezuela earthquakes has risen to 1,943, while the number of ‌injured ​almost doubled to ⁠10,571. The number of official rescues has drastically dropped in the last three days. The Venezuelan government said 5,380 people were saved in the first...
body: Death toll in Venezuela earthquakes has risen to 1,943, while the number of ‌injured ​almost doubled to ⁠10,571. The number of official rescues has drastically dropped in the last three days. The Venezuelan government said 5,380 people were saved in the first two days after the quakes, but on Monday, the authorities found only 4 people alive. More than 25 thousand firefighters, rescuers, police officers, soldiers, active and deployed brigadists are involved in attending to all those affected by the disaster. Earlier, on the 25th of June, two back-to-back powerful earthquakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 hit the South American nation. Multiple structures collapsed in Caracas following the strong shocks, which were felt across a large part of Venezuelan territory.

title: DRI busts cross-border gold smuggling racket in West Bengal; 24 kg foreign-origin gold seized
summary: The Directorate of Revenue Intelligence (DRI) has busted a cross-border gold smuggling racket operating along the Indo-Bangladesh border in West Bengal. DRI has seized around 24 kilograms of foreign-origin gold valued at approximately 34 crore rupees. Seven...
body: The Directorate of Revenue Intelligence (DRI) has busted a cross-border gold smuggling racket operating along the Indo-Bangladesh border in West Bengal. DRI has seized around 24 kilograms of foreign-origin gold valued at approximately 34 crore rupees. Seven people involved in the smuggling network have been arrested. According to the DRI, the seized gold, weighing nearly 24 kilograms, has been confiscated under the provisions of the Customs Act, 1962. Preliminary investigation has revealed that the carriers had collected the smuggled gold from locations near the Indo-Bangladesh border and were transporting the consignment for delivery to another set of carriers as part of a well-organised smuggling network. The DRI said the operation has dealt a significant blow to cross-border gold smuggling activities in the region. Further investigation is underway to identify other members of the syndicate and trace the source and intended destination of the smuggled gold.

title: Viksit Bharat G RAM G scheme comes into force across the country today, replacing existing MG NREGA
summary: Viksit Bharat-Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) [VB-G RAM G] Act, 2025 will come into force across rural India with effect from today. The Rural Development Ministry said that the act, which guarantees 125 days of wage employment in every...
body: Viksit Bharat-Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) [VB-G RAM G] Act, 2025 will come into force across rural India with effect from today. The Rural Development Ministry said that the act, which guarantees 125 days of wage employment in every financial year, will empower rural India. Our correspondent reports that a provision of 95,692 crore rupees has been made in the current financial year for the operationalisation of the new framework across the country. The VB-G RAM G Act replaces the twenty-year-old Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA), 2005. The new act aims to enhance the statutory employment guarantee from one hundred days to one hundred and twenty-five days in a financial year. This enhanced employment guarantee is intended to support the accelerated pace of rural development, provide greater income security, and empower rural households. The Act places strong emphasis on timely, transparent, and accountable wage payments. The new Act also provides for an unemployment allowance if work is not provided within the stipulated time. The Viksit Bharat-GRAM G Act is a comprehensive legislative framework designed to align rural employment generation with the national vision of Viksit Bharat by 2047. On the implementation, Union Minister of Rural Development Shivraj Singh Chouhan said, no eligible rural worker should remain without work even for a single day. Mr Chouhan said the Government, in close coordination with the States and Union Territories, has completed all administrative, financial and technical preparations to ensure a seamless transition to the new framework. The Minister said, adequate financial resources have been made available, implementation systems are fully in place and ongoing works will continue without interruption. He added, the enhanced guarantee of 125 days of wage employment will strengthen rural livelihoods, create durable community assets and accelerate the vision of Viksit Bharat.

title: India condemns demolition of historic 125-year-old Gurdwara in Pakistan
summary: India has strongly condemned the demolition of the historic 125-year-old sacred Gurdwara Sri Guru Singh Sabha Sahib in Farooqabad, Pakistan, calling it highly deplorable and targeted act of vandalism. In response to media queries regarding the reported...
body: India has strongly condemned the demolition of the historic 125-year-old sacred Gurdwara Sri Guru Singh Sabha Sahib in Farooqabad, Pakistan, calling it highly deplorable and targeted act of vandalism. In response to media queries regarding the reported demolition, External Affairs Ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal expressed grave concern over its destruction, along with reports of no meaningful action being taken by local authorities or the Evacuee Trust Property Board. He pointed out that this is not an isolated incident, as there have been similar reports earlier. The spokesperson said the systemic targeting of religious minorities and their places of worship in Pakistan continues unabated. India has called upon Pakistan to expeditiously investigate this matter and bring the perpetrators of this act to justice. Mr Jaiswal stressed that the demolished portions of the Gurdwara Sahib should be restored and reconstructed at the earliest. New Delhi has also urged Islamabad to discharge its obligations to ensure the safety, security, and well-being of its minority communities and their places of worship, and put a decisive end to the prevailing environment of sectarian violence and religious intolerance in Pakistan.

title: Akashvani Launches Broadcasting House Photo Gallery ‘Swar Prerna Veethika’
summary: Akashvani today unveiled its Broadcasting House Photo Gallery Swar Prerana Veethika at Rang Bhavan Auditorium in Akashvani Bhawan, New Delhi. The photo gallery was inaugurated by Padma Vibhushan Flautist, Pandit Hariprasad Chaurasia. The gallery features...
body: Akashvani today unveiled its Broadcasting House Photo Gallery Swar Prerana Veethika at Rang Bhavan Auditorium in Akashvani Bhawan, New Delhi. The photo gallery was inaugurated by Padma Vibhushan Flautist, Pandit Hariprasad Chaurasia. The gallery features portraits of Bharat Ratna awardees including Vidushi M.S. Subbulakshmi, Ustad Bismillah Khan, Pt. Bhimsen Joshi, Pt. Ravi Shankar, Lata Mangeshkar, and Dr. Bhupen Hazarika. During the event, a musical concert was organised in which a flute recital was performed by Pandit Rupak Kulkarni. During the event, Chief Executive Officer of Prasar Bharati, Gaurav Dwivedi and Director General of Akashvani and DD News, Rajiv Kumar Jain were present. Addressing the gathering, Mr Dwivedi said that Akashvani has played a pivotal role in preserving and promoting India’s rich musical heritage. Mr Dwivedi noted that Akashvani continues to provide a platform for Indian classical music, Carnatic music, Sugan music, folk music, devotional music, film music, Rabindra Sangeet, and many other genres of music through its dedicated channels, strengthening India’s cultural identity and unity.

title: Tanzania President praises India’s role in Zanzibar’s development
summary: Tanzania’s President Hussein Ali Mwinyi commended India’s vital role in Zanzibar’s development during his meeting with the High Commissioner of India to Tanzania, Bishwadip Dey, yesterday. President Mwinyi received High Commissioner Dey at the State House in...
body: Tanzania’s President Hussein Ali Mwinyi commended India’s vital role in Zanzibar’s development during his meeting with the High Commissioner of India to Tanzania, Bishwadip Dey, yesterday. President Mwinyi received High Commissioner Dey at the State House in Zanzibar and commended India’s vital role in Zanzibar’s development, particularly in investments, vocational training, and ICT innovation through IIT Madras Zanzibar. During this, the Indian High Commissioner extended an official invitation from India’s Vice President, C. Р. Radhakrishnan, for President Mwinyi to visit India in July, including a visit to the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Madras. The High Commissioner also participated in an Orientation Session for the ICCR Scholarship awardees from Zanzibar for the academic year 2026-27, organised by the consulate. He provided an overview of the India-Tanzania relationship and of educational and cultural cooperation between the two countries, including ICCR scholarships.

title: India, Malaysia review entire spectrum of bilateral military cooperation during 12th Sub Committee Meeting
summary: India and Malaysia reviewed entire spectrum of bilateral military cooperation during 12th Sub-Committee Meeting in New Delhi today. Defence Ministry in a statement, said that the two sides reaffirmed shared commitment to peace, security and freedom of...
body: India and Malaysia reviewed entire spectrum of bilateral military cooperation during 12th Sub-Committee Meeting in New Delhi today. Defence Ministry in a statement, said that the two sides reaffirmed shared commitment to peace, security and freedom of navigation in Indo-Pacific. The discussions covered ongoing defence engagements, including military-to-military exchanges, bilateral exercises, training, staff talks, capacity building, maritime cooperation, and cooperation in emerging domains. The Sub Committee Meeting on Military Cooperation was co-chaired by Joint Secretary of Ministry of Defence Amitabh Prasad and Assistant Chief of Staff, Defence Operations and Training, Malaysian Armed Forces Major General Amer Mahmud Bin Abdul Rahman. The meeting underscored the importance of strengthening defence cooperation as a key pillar of the India-Malaysia Enhanced Strategic Partnership. The Sub Committee Meeting serves as the principal military-to-military consultative mechanism between the two countries and is the precursor to the India-Malaysia Defence Cooperation Meeting held at the level of Defence Secretary. The meeting reviewed the progress made since the last SCMC and expressed confidence that the outcomes would provide a strong foundation for productive deliberations at the forthcoming India-Malaysia Defence Cooperation Meeting. The visiting delegation also called on Defence Secretary Rajesh Kumar Singh and visited the Defence Public Sector Undertakings (DPSU) Bhawan at World Trade Centre, New Delhi. Prior to the meeting, the head of the Malaysian delegation paid homage to India’s fallen heroes by laying a wreath at the National War Memorial in New Delhi.

title: Indian Naval Ships Tarkash and Ikshak conclude port call at Port Victoria, Seychelles
summary: Indian Naval Ships (INS) Tarkash and Ikshak concluded their port call at Port Victoria, Seychelles. Defence Ministry today said that the visit of the ships reaffirms the Indian Navy’s commitment to strengthening maritime cooperation with friendly maritime...
body: Indian Naval Ships (INS) Tarkash and Ikshak concluded their port call at Port Victoria, Seychelles. Defence Ministry today said that the visit of the ships reaffirms the Indian Navy’s commitment to strengthening maritime cooperation with friendly maritime nations in the Indian Ocean Region, in consonance with Mutual and Holistic Advancement for Security and Growth Across Regions (MAHASAGAR). It marked the successful completion of a range of professional, ceremonial and community engagement activities undertaken during Seychelles’ National Day and Golden Jubilee celebrations. During the visit, the marching contingent and Naval Band from INS Tarkash participated in the National Day Parade held on the 28th of June, reflecting the close and long-standing maritime partnership between India and Seychelles. Meanwhile, INS Ikshak hosted over 300 visitors, providing an opportunity to showcase the ship’s hydrographic survey capabilities and the Indian Navy’s contribution towards hydrographic cooperation in the Indian Ocean Region. The deployment underscores the Indian Navy’s continued focus on enhancing interoperability, capacity building and maritime security.

title: Hockey India announces Indian Junior Men’s team for Belgium tour
summary: Hockey India has announced the 22-member Indian Junior (Under-21) Men’s Hockey Team for the upcoming exposure tour of Belgium, scheduled from 5th to 18th of July. The tour will be the team’s first assignment under newly appointed coach Frederic Soyez and will...
body: Hockey India has announced the 22-member Indian Junior (Under-21) Men’s Hockey Team for the upcoming exposure tour of Belgium, scheduled from 5th to 18th of July. The tour will be the team’s first assignment under newly appointed coach Frederic Soyez and will serve as a key preparatory phase ahead of the FIH Hockey Men’s Junior Asia Cup later this year. Defender Anmol Ekka has been entrusted with the captaincy for the upcoming exposure tour. The goalkeeping unit comprises Vivek Lakra and Kunal Tewatia. The defensive line features captain Anmol Ekka, Rohit Kullu, Chirag, Ravinder, Prasant Barla, Sanjit Tirkey and V. Manimaran. India will begin their campaign against Austria on the 7th and 8th of July. The team will then take on the hosts, Belgium, on the 10th of July; Germany on the 13th of July; Belgium again on the 14th of July; and conclude the tour against the Netherlands on the 17th of July.

title: India to take on England in first T20I of five-match series today
summary: In Men’s Cricket, India will take on England in the first T20 International of the 5-match series at the Riverside Ground in Chester-le-Street today. The match is scheduled to begin at 10 PM Indian Time. Shreyas Iyer-led Indian side will look to bounce back...
body: In Men’s Cricket, India will take on England in the first T20 International of the 5-match series at the Riverside Ground in Chester-le-Street today. The match is scheduled to begin at 10 PM Indian Time. Shreyas Iyer-led Indian side will look to bounce back from their shocking 2-0 defeat to Ireland in the last series. All eyes will be on young sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, who received his maiden senior Indian Team call-up for the Ireland series but did not get a debut in Belfast. Meanwhile, the second T20I will be played at Manchester on Saturday, while the third match will be at Nottingham on the 7th of July. Bristol and Southampton will host the final two matches of the T20 series, which concludes on the 11th of July. Following this, India will also face England in a three-match ODI series.

title: NTA releases admit cards for ICAR PG and PhD entrance exams
summary: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has released the admit cards for the Indian Council of Agricultural Research’s All India Entrance Examination for Admission (AIEEA)-PG and the All India Competitive Examination (AICE)-JRF/SRF (Ph.D.) 2026, scheduled to be...
body: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has released the admit cards for the Indian Council of Agricultural Research’s All India Entrance Examination for Admission (AIEEA)-PG and the All India Competitive Examination (AICE)-JRF/SRF (Ph.D.) 2026, scheduled to be held on the 4th of July. The AIEEA-PG examination will be conducted in the morning shift, from 10 AM to 12 noon, while the AICE-JRF/SRF (Ph.D.) examination will take place in the afternoon shift, from 3 PM to 5 PM. Both examinations will be held in Computer-Based Test (CBT) mode. Candidates can download their admit cards using their login credentials, and check the examination schedule on the official website. The NTA has advised candidates to regularly visit the official websites, www.nta.ac.in and exams.nta.nic.in/icar/ for the latest updates. NTA also said that candidates facing difficulty in downloading their admit cards may contact the NTA Helpdesk at 011-40759000 or 011-69227700, or send an email to icar@nta.ac.in.

title: Gold rises 0.5%, silver slips 0.1% in Indian bullion market
summary: In the Indian bullion market, 24-carat gold was trading half a per cent up at 143,610 rupees per 10 grams, while Silver was trading 0.1 per cent down at 228,550 rupees per kilogram a short while ago. At the Multi Commodity Exchange, Gold for August contract...
body: In the Indian bullion market, 24-carat gold was trading half a per cent up at 143,610 rupees per 10 grams, while Silver was trading 0.1 per cent down at 228,550 rupees per kilogram a short while ago. At the Multi Commodity Exchange, Gold for August contract was trading half a per cent up at 143,200 rupees per 10 grams, and Silver for September contract was trading marginally lower at 228,421 rupees per kilogram, when reports last came in.

title: JPC Chief Chaudhary stresses need for holding simultaneous elections to Lok Sabha, State Legislative Assemblies
summary: Chairperson of the Joint Parliamentary Committee on One Nation, One Election, P.P. Chaudhary, today stressed the need for holding simultaneous elections to the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies. Addressing a press conference in New Delhi, he said...
body: Chairperson of the Joint Parliamentary Committee on One Nation, One Election, P.P. Chaudhary, today stressed the need for holding simultaneous elections to the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies. Addressing a press conference in New Delhi, he said conducting elections together would strengthen governance, reduce disruptions caused by frequent polls and benefit the economy. Mr Chaudhary said the concept of simultaneous elections is not new and has been recommended by the Election Commission, the Law Commission and other expert bodies over the years. He said elections to the Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies were held simultaneously during the initial decades after Independence, but the electoral cycle was disrupted following the premature dissolution of legislatures and the imposition of President’s Rule in several states. Highlighting the benefits of One Nation, One Election, Mr Chaudhary said that it would reduce the repeated enforcement of the Model Code of Conduct.

title: ICAR, Agriculture Department of Seychelles sign MoU to enhance collaboration in agricultural research
summary: The Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) and the Agriculture Department of Republic of Seychelles signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) during the official visit of Prime Minister Narendra Modi to Seychelles. The Agriculture and Farmers Welfare...
body: The Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) and the Agriculture Department of Republic of Seychelles signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) during the official visit of Prime Minister Narendra Modi to Seychelles. The Agriculture and Farmers Welfare Ministry said that to operationalise the agreement, both sides also signed a five-year Work Plan from 2026 to 2031 to focus on climate-smart agriculture, horticulture, livestock, post-harvest management and food security. The Ministry added that the MoU was signed to enhance collaboration in agricultural research, education, capacity building and technology transfer between the two countries. The agreement provides a framework for promoting joint research programmes, exchange of scientists, researchers and students, and sharing of advanced agricultural technologies and best practices. The collaboration aims to strengthen institutional linkages, improve agricultural productivity, build technical capacity, and support sustainable agricultural development while addressing emerging challenges such as climate change and food security.

title: Health Minister Nadda reviews TB Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan and dengue preparedness
summary: Union Health Minister Jagat Prakash Nadda has called for enhanced public participation to accelerate the TB Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan during a review meeting with the Delhi Government on the campaign and dengue preparedness. The Minister stressed that all...
body: Union Health Minister Jagat Prakash Nadda has called for enhanced public participation to accelerate the TB Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan during a review meeting with the Delhi Government on the campaign and dengue preparedness. The Minister stressed that all campaign-related communication should be in simple and easy-to-understand language to ensure wider public outreach. He directed that public representatives should be actively associated with the campaign and said every ward should be monitored by senior government officials for effective implementation. Referring to Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s guidance during the recent PRAGATI meeting, Mr. Nadda also called for greater involvement of MY Bharat volunteers to strengthen grassroots mobilisation. Highlighting the role of technology, the Union Health Minister said support will be provided for more AI-enabled handheld X-ray machines to facilitate early TB diagnosis and improve case detection. He also underlined the need to involve Resident Welfare Associations (RWAs), with clearly defined roles in both the TB Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan and dengue preparedness. The meeting also reviewed the Delhi Government’s preparedness for dengue, with a focus on coordinated action, effective surveillance, public awareness and timely preventive measures.

title: Rupee depreciates by 57 paise to close at 95.24 against US dollar
summary: In the forex market today, the rupee depreciated by 57 paise to close at 95 rupees and 24 paise against the US dollar. The dollar index, which indicates the US dollar’s strength against a basket of six other leading currencies, was trading at 101.37 in...
body: In the forex market today, the rupee depreciated by 57 paise to close at 95 rupees and 24 paise against the US dollar. The dollar index, which indicates the US dollar’s strength against a basket of six other leading currencies, was trading at 101.37 in intra-day trade today.

title: Union Minister Piyush Goyal discusses strengthening India-Greece economic ties
summary: Union Minister for Commerce and Industry Piyush Goyal has discussed strengthening India-Greece economic ties by enhancing cooperation in financial services, fintech, digital payments, and emerging technologies. He met Greek Minister of Economy and Finance...
body: Union Minister for Commerce and Industry Piyush Goyal has discussed strengthening India-Greece economic ties by enhancing cooperation in financial services, fintech, digital payments, and emerging technologies. He met Greek Minister of Economy and Finance Kyriakos Pierrakakis in Greece. The two sides also explored avenues for deeper collaboration between Indian and Greek institutions and businesses to foster shared prosperity. Mr. Goyal also met Greek Minister for Infrastructure and Transport Christos Dimas, discussing enhanced India-Greece cooperation across infrastructure, transport connectivity, the maritime sector, aviation, and sustainable mobility. He looked forward to deepening collaboration to build resilient supply chains and further strengthen connectivity between India and Europe.

title: Australia enter ICC Women’s T20 World Cup final with eight-wicket win over West Indies
summary: In the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup, Australia stormed into the final today after defeating the West Indies by eight wickets at The Oval in London. Batting first, the West Indies were restricted to 125 for 7 in their allotted 20 overs. In reply, Australia chased...
body: In the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup, Australia stormed into the final today after defeating the West Indies by eight wickets at The Oval in London. Batting first, the West Indies were restricted to 125 for 7 in their allotted 20 overs. In reply, Australia chased down the target in just 13 overs, losing only two wickets. The unbeaten Australians have now reached their eighth final in 10 editions of the tournament and will be aiming to win a record seventh Women’s T20 World Cup title. Australia will face either hosts England or South Africa in the final at Lord’s on Sunday. England and South Africa will meet in the second semi-final tomorrow at The Oval.

title: Lt Gen Sandeep Jain assumes charge as Vice Chief of Army Staff
summary: Lieutenant General Sandeep Jain today assumed the appointment of the Vice Chief of the Army Staff. Lieutenant General Jain is an alumnus of the National Defence Academy, and was commissioned into the MAHAR Regiment in June 1988. His operational experience...
body: Lieutenant General Sandeep Jain today assumed the appointment of the Vice Chief of the Army Staff. Lieutenant General Jain is an alumnus of the National Defence Academy, and was commissioned into the MAHAR Regiment in June 1988. His operational experience includes participation in Operation Pawan, service as a Military Observer with the United Nations Mission in Ethiopia and multiple tenures in high-altitude areas and counter-insurgency operations along the Line of Control and in the North East. The General Officer also commanded a Sector in the UN Mission in South Sudan. On promotion to Lieutenant General, he commanded a Corps in Northern Command. He later served as Chief of Staff at Headquarters Southern Command, where he contributed significantly to capability development, force restructuring and overall operational preparedness. On elevation as Army Commander, he served as General Officer Commanding-in-Chief, Southern Command.

title: Govt approves construction of Six-lane Dwarka tunnel in Delhi
summary: The Government today approved the construction of 6 Lane Road Tunnel For NH-148AE connecting Dwarka Expressway with Nelson Mandela Marg, Vasant Kunj in Delhi. Briefing the media in New Delhi today, Information and Broadcasting Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw said...
body: The Government today approved the construction of 6 Lane Road Tunnel For NH-148AE connecting Dwarka Expressway with Nelson Mandela Marg, Vasant Kunj in Delhi. Briefing the media in New Delhi today, Information and Broadcasting Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw said that the 8.1 km project will be developed with a total capital cost of over 6,969 crore rupees. He said that the project will provide faster connectivity between West and South Delhi, connecting Urban Extension Road 2/Dwarka Expressway with Vasant Kunj in South Delhi. Mr Vaishnaw informed that an elevated road of 1.8 km is also proposed along the Nelson Mandela Marg to mitigate congestion at this intersection. He said that the National Highways Authority of India is also proposing an Elevated Corridor between AIIMS and Mahipalpur. He said that the project will generate close to 7.54 lakh person-days of direct employment and 9.80 lakh person-days of indirect employment. Mr Vaishnaw said that the government has also approved the construction of the 117.7 km 4/6 lane access-controlled Kanpur-Kabrai section of NH-34 in Uttar Pradesh. The project has an estimated total capital cost of 7,145 crore rupees. The Minister said that the project will provide seamless, high-speed connectivity between Kanpur and Kabrai while strengthening onward connectivity to Sagar, Bhopal and other parts of Madhya Pradesh. He highlighted that the project will also provide strategic connectivity with NH-34, NH-35, the Bundelkhand Expressway, Kanpur Ring Road, and State Highways SH-46, SH-91, SH-10B and SH-42, thereby strengthening integration with the regional highway network. Designed for operating speeds of 80 to 100 kmph, the corridor will reduce travel time between Kanpur and Kabrai from 3.5 hours to 1.5 hours. Mr Vaishnaw said that the project will generate close to 1.2 Crore person-days of direct and indirect employment.

title: India and Pakistan Exchange Lists of Prisoners
summary: India and Pakistan today exchanged lists of civil prisoners and fishermen in each other’s custody through diplomatic channels in New Delhi and Islamabad. Such lists are exchanged every year on 1st January and 1st July under the provisions of the Agreement on...
body: India and Pakistan today exchanged lists of civil prisoners and fishermen in each other’s custody through diplomatic channels in New Delhi and Islamabad. Such lists are exchanged every year on 1st January and 1st July under the provisions of the Agreement on Consular Access signed between India and Pakistan in 2008. The External Affairs Ministry said in a statement that India has shared lists of 386 civil prisoners and 53 fishermen in its custody, who are Pakistani or believed-to-be-Pakistani. Pakistan has shared lists of 52 civil prisoners and 198 fishermen in its custody, who are Indian or believed-to-be-Indian. India has consistently called for the early release and repatriation of civil prisoners, fishermen along with their boats, and missing Indian defence personnel from Pakistan’s custody. Pakistan has been urged to expedite the release and repatriation of 188 Indian fishermen and civil prisoners, who have completed their sentences. Pakistan has been asked to provide immediate consular access to the 13 civil prisoners in Pakistan’s custody, who are believed-to-be-Indian and have not been provided consular access so far. The Indian government has urged Pakistan government to ensure the safety, security and welfare of all Indian and believed-to-be-Indian prisoners pending their release and repatriation to India. As a result of sustained efforts by India, 2,661 Indian fishermen and 78 Indian civil prisoners have been repatriated from Pakistan since 2014. These include 500 Indian fishermen and 20 Indian civil prisoners who have been repatriated from Pakistan since 2023 till date.

title: Sensex jumps 444 points, Nifty closes above 24,000
summary: Benchmark domestic equity indices ended 0.6 per cent higher today, snapping a two session losing streak. The Sensex rose 444 points to close at 76 thousand 923. The Nifty also gained 140 points to settle at 24 thousand and six. In the broader market on the...
body: Benchmark domestic equity indices ended 0.6 per cent higher today, snapping a two session losing streak. The Sensex rose 444 points to close at 76 thousand 923. The Nifty also gained 140 points to settle at 24 thousand and six. In the broader market on the National Stock Exchange, the Midcap 100 index added over 0.3 per cent and the Smallcap 100 index climbed around 0.4 per cent. Earlier, markets opened marginally higher. Both the indices extended their upward momentum throughout the session supported by softer crude oil prices and buying across specific sectors. In the Sensex pack, shares of 21 out of 30 companies registered gains. Among the top performers, Eternal soared 5.7 per cent, Asian Paints surged 3.1 per cent and Hindustan Unilever jumped three per cent. Conversely, HCL Tech shed almost three and a half per cent, Tech Mahindra dropped nearly 3.1 per cent, and Tata Consultancy Services lost over two and a half per cent. In the sectoral indices on the BSE, 15 out of 25 sectors ended in positive zone. Among the key gainers, Realty climbed 3.6 per cent, FMCG rose 1.8 per cent and Services added over 1.3 per cent. In contrast, Focused IT fell two and a half per cent, IT lost 1.9 per cent and Metal slipped 0.9 per cent. The overall market breadth at the BSE was positive, as shares of 2 thousand 276 companies advanced, 1 thousand 995 declined, and 176 remained unchanged.

title: EAM Jaishankar receives Ambassador of UAE Dr. Abdulnasser Alshaali on a farewell call
summary: External Affairs Minister Dr S. Jaishankar received the Ambassador of the United Arab Emirates Dr. Abdulnasser Alshaali, today on a farewell call. In a social media post, Dr Jaishankar thanked the Ambassador for his efforts towards deepening the India-UAE...
body: External Affairs Minister Dr S. Jaishankar received the Ambassador of the United Arab Emirates Dr. Abdulnasser Alshaali, today on a farewell call. In a social media post, Dr Jaishankar thanked the Ambassador for his efforts towards deepening the India-UAE Comprehensive Strategic Partnership. He also wished the UAE Ambassador all the best in his future endeavours.

title: Govt appoints Somnath Ghosh as next High Commissioner of India to Malaysia
summary: The Government has appointed Somnath Ghosh as the next High Commissioner of India to Malaysia. Mr. Ghosh, an Indian Foreign Service officer of the 1997 batch, is presently serving as Consul General of India in Chicago. The Ministry of External Affairs said...
body: The Government has appointed Somnath Ghosh as the next High Commissioner of India to Malaysia. Mr. Ghosh, an Indian Foreign Service officer of the 1997 batch, is presently serving as Consul General of India in Chicago. The Ministry of External Affairs said that he is expected to take up the assignment shortly.

title: HM Shah asks High-Level Committee on Demographic Changes to submit its recommendations at the earliest
summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah has asked the High-Level Committee on Demographic Changes to submit its recommendations at the earliest. Mr Shah gave the direction during a meeting with the Committee in New Delhi. The Committee informed the Home Minister that...
body: Union Home Minister Amit Shah has asked the High-Level Committee on Demographic Changes to submit its recommendations at the earliest. Mr Shah gave the direction during a meeting with the Committee in New Delhi. The Committee informed the Home Minister that they will visit various States and Union Territories to obtain first-hand information on demographic changes and interact with the various administrations. They will also hold consultations with central government ministries to gather feedback on issues related to demographic changes. The Committee informed the Home Minister that a detailed questionnaire has been prepared to seek relevant information from States and Union Territories in advance, making its field visits more meaningful and interactive. Appreciating the Committee’s strategy, Mr Shah directed the Union Home Secretary to provide all possible assistance for its day-to-day functioning as well as during its visits to States and Union Territories. Prime Minister Narendra Modi had announced the High-Powered Demography Mission on 15th August last year. The Government constituted the High-Level Committee to study demographic changes arising from illegal immigration and other abnormal reasons, analyse their causes, and recommend appropriate policy, legislative, and administrative measures. Retired Justice Prakash Prabhakar Naolekar Is the Chairman of the Committee.

title: Ishan Kishan replaces Abhishek Sharma as world’s Number one T20 International batter
summary: India wicketkeeper-batter Ishan Kishan has replaced teammate Abhishek Sharma as the world’s Number one T20 International batter in the latest ICC Player Rankings. Kishan attained a new career-best rating after the latest ICC rankings update released today,...
body: India wicketkeeper-batter Ishan Kishan has replaced teammate Abhishek Sharma as the world’s Number one T20 International batter in the latest ICC Player Rankings. Kishan attained a new career-best rating after the latest ICC rankings update released today, following India’s 2-nil T20 International series defeat to Ireland in Belfast. The left-handed batter’s rise to the summit has been fuelled by his outstanding performances at the ICC T20 World Cup earlier this year, where he amassed 317 runs at a strike rate of almost 200. In another major change, Australia’s Travis Head climbed to the Number one spot among Test batters for the first time in his career.

title: BJP criticises Telangana CM Reddy over remarks against BJP National President Nabin
summary: The Bharatiya Janata Party-BJP today criticised Telangana Chief Minister Revanth Reddy over his alleged objectionable remarks against the BJP National President, Nitin Nabin. Addressing the media in New Delhi, BJP spokesperson Sambit Patra said the language...
body: The Bharatiya Janata Party-BJP today criticised Telangana Chief Minister Revanth Reddy over his alleged objectionable remarks against the BJP National President, Nitin Nabin. Addressing the media in New Delhi, BJP spokesperson Sambit Patra said the language used by the Telangana Chief Minister against Mr Nabin is highly derogatory and condemnable and reflects the political culture of the Congress party. Mr Patra added that Mr Nabin is a five-time MLA and has also served as a Cabinet Minister in Bihar. He said the elevation of a grassroots leader to the post of the National President is possible only in the Bharatiya Janata Party.

title: Australia to begin Indian Film Festival of Melbourne celebrations with Lagaan screening on July 9
summary: Australia is all set to roll out the red carpet as the Indian Film Festival of Melbourne takes centre stage from 13th to 23th of August. This year, the festival will kick off its celebrations with a special curtain raiser event on 9 July, with a screening of...
body: Australia is all set to roll out the red carpet as the Indian Film Festival of Melbourne takes centre stage from 13th to 23th of August. This year, the festival will kick off its celebrations with a special curtain raiser event on 9 July, with a screening of the landmark film Lagaan at the iconic ACMI cinema. The occasion commemorates 25 years of the Academy Award-nominated classic Lagaan. The Indian Film Festival of Melbourne has emerged as a major international platform for Indian cinema over the years. The 17th edition of the festival will feature film screenings, premieres, masterclasses and cultural events, bringing together filmmakers, actors, industry professionals and cinema enthusiasts from around the world.

title: Air Marshal Tejinder Singh takes over as Chief of Integrated Defence Staff
summary: Air Marshal Tejinder Singh today took over as the Chief of Integrated Defence Staff to Chairman Chiefs of Staff Committee (CISC). As he assumed his new appointment, the Air Marshal laid a wreath at the National War Memorial, New Delhi and paid homage to the...
body: Air Marshal Tejinder Singh today took over as the Chief of Integrated Defence Staff to Chairman Chiefs of Staff Committee (CISC). As he assumed his new appointment, the Air Marshal laid a wreath at the National War Memorial, New Delhi and paid homage to the bravehearts who made the supreme sacrifice in the service of the nation. He was also accorded a ceremonial tri-service Guard of Honour. The Defence Ministry said that Air Marshal Tejinder Singh was commissioned into the fighter stream of the Indian Air Force in June 1987 and has flying experience of over 4,500 hours. He has commanded a Fighter Squadron, a Radar Station, a premier Fighter Base and was Air Officer Commanding, Jammu and Kashmir. In recognition of his meritorious service, Air Marshal Tejinder Singh was awarded the Vayu Sena Medal in 2007, Ati Vishisht Seva Medal in 2022 and Param Vishisht Seva Medal in 2026.

title: Union Ministers Shivraj Singh Chouhan & Kiren Rijiju undertake aerial survey of flood-hit areas of Arunachal Pradesh
summary: On the second day of their high-level assessment tour, Union Ministers Shivraj Singh Chouhan and Kiren Rijiju, accompanied by Arunachal Pradesh Chief Minister Pema Khandu, conducted an aerial survey of flood-hit areas in the state today. The team first...
body: On the second day of their high-level assessment tour, Union Ministers Shivraj Singh Chouhan and Kiren Rijiju, accompanied by Arunachal Pradesh Chief Minister Pema Khandu, conducted an aerial survey of flood-hit areas in the state today. The team first surveyed Pasighat in East Siang district, one of the worst-affected regions, before visiting Boying village to assess the damage on the ground. Flash floods and landslides have submerged more than 20 houses in the village and caused extensive damage to paddy fields, standing crops and livestock, leaving many families reeling from the devastation. Union Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan interacted closely with the displaced villagers whose homes were destroyed. Reassuring the community, Chouhan promised comprehensive financial and structural assistance from the Central Government, executing relief operations in close synchronization with the state administration. He also praised the resilience of the Boying village community, expressing deep admiration for the local volunteers who proactively engineered a temporary wall to hold back the advancing floodwaters. Addressing long-term prevention, officials confirmed that the construction of a permanent dam to safeguard Pasighat town from recurring river overflows is currently under active discussion between state and central bodies. Following their assessment in East Siang, the high-level delegation departed for Yazali in the Lower Subansiri district to evaluate the flood crisis in that region. So far, the death toll is four, while 21 others were injured in the state, where 90,499 people were affected in almost all 28 districts. Upper Siang is the worst-hit district, with 47,357 people affected, followed by Siang with 23,715, Kra Daadi with 8,171 and East Kameng with 5,895. The convoy next traveled to the NEEPCO colony in the newly formed Keyi Panyor district, the epicentre where the devastating flash floods first struck on June 24. The area has suffered irreparable damage, with entire rows of houses and vital road networks completely washed away. Addressing the affected families and displaced residents at the site, Minister Chouhan expressed solidarity with the victims and assured them of continued support. He said the Central Government, under the leadership of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, will work in close coordination with the state government to help the affected communities recover and rebuild. The ministers also visited temporary relief camps set up inside the NEEPCO colony, spending time with the displaced families and urging them not to lose hope. Following their evaluation in Arunachal Pradesh, Union Ministers Chouhan and Rijiju crossed over into neighbouring Assam to inspect flood-ravaged sectors in that state. After completing their multi-state aerial and ground damage assessments, the Union Ministers are scheduled to depart for New Delhi from Guwahati this evening to submit their findings to the central leadership.

title: PM Modi says, Eleven years of Digital India empowered the poor & underprivileged, making life easier for millions
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi today said that the Digital India initiative has redefined governance, empowered citizens and accelerated all-around development. Marking the 11 years of the initiative, Mr Modi said that Digital India has touched every aspect of...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi today said that the Digital India initiative has redefined governance, empowered citizens and accelerated all-around development. Marking the 11 years of the initiative, Mr Modi said that Digital India has touched every aspect of life. He said that the initiative has played a pivotal role in empowering the poor and the underprivileged while making life easier for the citizens. From the expansion of the optical fiber network to the rise of digital transactions, the unprecedented success of this initiative has drawn global attention to India. He underscored that seamless digital payments and direct benefit transfers are reaching beneficiaries with transparency. Mr Modi said that technology has become a powerful instrument for furthering Ease of Living. He underlined that Digital India has taken the wave of innovation to all parts of India, especially villages, Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities. Mr Modi said that Young entrepreneurs, start-ups, and innovators from every corner of the country are creating solutions to the pressing challenges that our planet faces. He further highlighted that this initiative has strengthened education, healthcare, agriculture, commerce and public service delivery. The Prime Minister said that government strides in the digital world have ensured that India is making rapid progress in emerging technologies such as AI, semiconductors, quantum computing and more. He added that the government’s focus will remain on creating a future where technology serves humanity, empowers every citizen and drives sustainable development.

title: Increased wages under Viksit Bharat G RAM G Act become effective from today
summary: The Central Government has notified revised wage rates under the Viksit Bharat G RAM G Act, 2025, effective from today. According to the notification issued by the Rural Development Ministry, wage rates have been enhanced across all States and Union...
body: The Central Government has notified revised wage rates under the Viksit Bharat G RAM G Act, 2025, effective from today. According to the notification issued by the Rural Development Ministry, wage rates have been enhanced across all States and Union Territories. It said for the first time, an interim base wage rate of three hundred rupees per day has been introduced, ensuring that no notified wage under the programme falls below this level. The national average wage has risen from 298.8 rupees per day under MGNREGA to 327.4 rupees per day under VB-G RAM G, marking an average increase of over 10%. Twenty-one States and administrative units, which previously had wages below 300 rupees, have now been brought up to the new benchmark. The States of Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Jharkhand, West Bengal, Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, and Himachal Pradesh are among those witnessing significant hikes ranging between 15 and 25%. The Ministry highlighted that Arunachal Pradesh and Nagaland have received the highest percentage increase of nearly 24.5%. States with already higher wages have also seen upward revisions. Haryana will now have a wage rate of 409 rupees, Goa 406 rupees, and Kerala 401 rupees per day. In Sikkim’s High Altitude Gram Panchayats, the rate has been set at 450 rupees per day. Union Minister for Rural Development Shivraj Singh Chouhan described the revision as a historic step towards building a Viksit Bharat through prosperous villages.

title: Air Marshal Ashutosh Dixit assumes charge as Vice Chief of Air Staff
summary: Air Marshal Ashutosh Dixit today assumed charge as the Vice Chief of the Air Staff of the Indian Air Force. He succeeds Air Marshal Nagesh Kapoor, who retired yesterday after four decades of service. During his nearly four-decade-long career, Air Marshal...
body: Air Marshal Ashutosh Dixit today assumed charge as the Vice Chief of the Air Staff of the Indian Air Force. He succeeds Air Marshal Nagesh Kapoor, who retired yesterday after four decades of service. During his nearly four-decade-long career, Air Marshal Dixit has participated in several key operations and exercises, including Operation Safed Sagar, Operation Rakshak, Cope India and Operation Sindoor. He has held several important operational and staff appointments, including Air Officer Commanding-in-Chief of the Central Air Command, Deputy Chief of the Air Staff and Chief of Integrated Defence Staff to the Chairman, Chiefs of Staff Committee. In recognition of his distinguished service, Air Marshal Dixit has been awarded the Vayu Sena Medal, Vishisht Seva Medal, Ati Vishisht Seva Medal and the Param Vishisht Seva Medal.

title: Afghanistan carries out airstrikes on ISIS-linked targets in Pakistan
summary: Afghanistan forces have carried out airstrikes against ISIS-linked targets in Pakistan. In a statement, the Afghan Ministry said the attacks were carried out last night against ISIS facilities in Pakistan’s Balochistan and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa provinces. The...
body: Afghanistan forces have carried out airstrikes against ISIS-linked targets in Pakistan. In a statement, the Afghan Ministry said the attacks were carried out last night against ISIS facilities in Pakistan’s Balochistan and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa provinces. The strikes inflicted heavy casualties and significant financial losses on ISIS and its supporters. It added that the attacks were carried out with high precision against designated targets and caused no civilian casualties. The strikes came just days after 36 civilians, including women and children, were killed, while 163 others were injured in Pakistani military airstrikes in Paktia, Paktika, and Kunar provinces of Afghanistan. Following these strikes by Pakistan, Afghanistan’s Permanent Mission has urged the United Nations and the wider international community to closely monitor the situation. The airstrikes have drawn widespread international concern. UN Secretary-General António Guterres called for an immediate cessation of hostilities and urged all parties to protect civilians. Earlier, Afghanistan’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs summoned the Chargé d’Affaires of Pakistan’s Embassy in Kabul to lodge a strong protest.

title: Ministry of Steel grants Miniratna Category-I status to MECON Limited
summary: The Ministry of Steel has approved the grant of Miniratna Category-I status to MECON Limited, a Schedule ‘A’ Central Public Sector Enterprise (CPSE) under the administrative control of the Ministry of Steel. MECON has achieved significant financial milestones...
body: The Ministry of Steel has approved the grant of Miniratna Category-I status to MECON Limited, a Schedule ‘A’ Central Public Sector Enterprise (CPSE) under the administrative control of the Ministry of Steel. MECON has achieved significant financial milestones in recent years and reported continuous profits during the last three financial years. The Company also reported a positive net worth of over 535 crore rupees as on 31 March this year, thereby fulfilling the eligibility criteria prescribed by the Department of Public Enterprises (DPE) for grant of Miniratna Category-I status.

title: Government refutes fake video claiming PM Modi announced free electric bicycles
summary: The government has refuted a video circulating on social media claiming that Prime Minister Narendra Modi has said that the government is providing free electric bicycles to children for going to school. The Fact Check Unit of the Press Information Bureau...
body: The government has refuted a video circulating on social media claiming that Prime Minister Narendra Modi has said that the government is providing free electric bicycles to children for going to school. The Fact Check Unit of the Press Information Bureau (PIB) has said that the video is Fake and AI- Generated. PIB has clarified that no such scheme or announcement has been made by the Government. The Unit has further advised citizens to rely only on official sources for information related to the Central Government’s public welfare schemes. It has also urged people not to be misled by such enticing claims and to refrain from forwarding unverified content.

title: FM Nirmala Sitharaman embarks on four-day official visit to France
summary: Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman has embarked on a four-day official visit to France today. The Finance Ministry has informed that Ms Sitharaman will participate in a series of high-level bilateral engagements aimed at further strengthening the...
body: Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman has embarked on a four-day official visit to France today. The Finance Ministry has informed that Ms Sitharaman will participate in a series of high-level bilateral engagements aimed at further strengthening the India-France Strategic Partnership, enhancing economic cooperation and promoting investment, technology collaboration and innovation. She will also co-chair the India-France Economic and Financial Dialogue (EFD) with her French counterpart, Roland Lescure, at Aix-en-Provence. The Ministry said that as part of the EFD, both countries will discuss potential areas for greater bilateral cooperation in various sectors and further enhance the economic relations between India and France. During the visit, the Union Finance Minister will hold one-to-one meetings with select global CEOs and also participate in a roundtable interaction with leading business executives to showcase India’s robust macroeconomic fundamentals, ongoing structural reforms, expanding investment opportunities and long-term growth prospects.

title: GST strengthens Ek Bharat Shreshtha Bharat vision over past nine years
summary: Goods and Services Tax (GST) has strengthened India’s vision of Ek Bharat – Shreshtha Bharat over the past nine years. It has brought transparency, accountability and economic growth through rationalised tax rates and standardised procedures. Launched on 1st...
body: Goods and Services Tax (GST) has strengthened India’s vision of Ek Bharat – Shreshtha Bharat over the past nine years. It has brought transparency, accountability and economic growth through rationalised tax rates and standardised procedures. Launched on 1st July 2017, this day is marked as a historic achievement in India’s reform journey. This day reflects India’s continued effort to create a simpler, more transparent and integrated indirect tax system. GST subsumed 17 different taxes and 13 cesses into a common framework. Reforms under GST 2.0 build on this progress by addressing the needs of citizens and businesses and supporting India’s progress towards a Viksit Bharat. The Next-Generation GST reforms of 2025 further simplified the structure through lower rates, exemptions and easier processes. These measures aim to benefit households, MSMEs, farmers, artisans, exporters and various trade sectors.

title: BJP claims Punjab govt implemented VB-G RAM G scheme after initial opposition
summary: BJP today claimed that the Aam Aadmi Party-led Punjab Government has implemented the Viksit Bharat G Ram G scheme after initially opposing it. Addressing the media in New Delhi, BJP spokesperson Prem Shukla questioned the Punjab Government’s sudden decision...
body: BJP today claimed that the Aam Aadmi Party-led Punjab Government has implemented the Viksit Bharat G Ram G scheme after initially opposing it. Addressing the media in New Delhi, BJP spokesperson Prem Shukla questioned the Punjab Government’s sudden decision to implement the center’s rural employment scheme. He alleged that the Bhagwant Mann Government is now praising VB G RAM G

title: President Droupadi Murmu calls upon youth to pursue excellence for Viksit Bharat 2047
summary: President Droupadi Murmu today called upon the youth to pursue excellence with dedication, integrity and strong ethical values to help realise the vision of a developed Bharat by 2047. Addressing the first convocation of the Central University of Andhra...
body: President Droupadi Murmu today called upon the youth to pursue excellence with dedication, integrity and strong ethical values to help realise the vision of a developed Bharat by 2047. Addressing the first convocation of the Central University of Andhra Pradesh at Anantapur as the Chief Guest, the President said the energy, talent and commitment of young people will play a decisive role in building an inclusive and prosperous nation. Congratulating the graduating students, the President said the convocation marks a defining milestone in the journey of the young university as well as in the lives of its first graduating batch. She noted that girls outperformed boys in securing the medals, reflecting the growing aspirations and determination of young women in the country. The President urged the graduates to continue learning throughout their lives, as rapid technological advancements are transforming every sector. She said acquiring a degree should not mark the end of learning and encouraged students to adapt to emerging technologies, explore new ideas and embrace innovation with perseverance. Emphasising the importance of values, the President said honesty, compassion, empathy and respect for nature are the foundation of sustainable and holistic development. She urged the students to use their knowledge for the benefit of society, particularly the underprivileged, whether they choose careers as entrepreneurs, scientists, public servants or in other fields. Highlighting the university’s vision, the President expressed confidence that it would emerge as a centre of excellence and contribute significantly to the national goal of a developed Bharat by 2047. She appreciated its commitment to quality education and sustainable development and said the presence of students from many states creates a vibrant learning environment that strengthens national integration through diversity. The President also recalled the rich heritage of the Anantapur district and its association with former Presidents Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan, who taught at the Government College in Anantapur, and Dr. Neelam Sanjiva Reddy, who hailed from the district. President Droupadi Murmu conferred degrees on 1,268 students who successfully completed undergraduate and postgraduate programmes and presented gold medals to 56 students for their outstanding academic performance. Andhra Pradesh Governor S. Abdul Nazeer, State Education Minister Nara Lokesh, State Finance Minister P. Keshav, University Chancellor Dr. Shankar Acharya, Vice-Chancellor S. A. Kori and other dignitaries attended the convocation.

title: HM Amit Shah says India transformed into digital nation through expanded connectivity
summary: Home Minister Amit Shah has said that the government has transformed India into a digital nation by expanding digital connectivity, strengthening digital governance, and improving access to public services. Marking 11 years of the Digital India initiative, Mr...
body: Home Minister Amit Shah has said that the government has transformed India into a digital nation by expanding digital connectivity, strengthening digital governance, and improving access to public services. Marking 11 years of the Digital India initiative, Mr Shah said the country has witnessed significant progress in internet connectivity, digital infrastructure, and technology-driven governance. In a social media post, he said that over 103 crore people are now connected to the internet, while more than 2.20 lakh villages have been linked through BharatNet. The Minister added that Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) has enabled the transfer of over 51 lakh crore rupees to beneficiaries, and India now accounts for nearly half of the world’s digital transactions. Mr Shah further said digitalisation has become an integral part of the country’s development journey, driving innovation, supporting startups, and boosting emerging sectors such as artificial intelligence, semiconductors, and electronics manufacturing.

title: GST collections rise 13.9% to ₹1.94 lakh crore in June
summary: Goods and Services Tax (GST) collections in the last month witnessed a rise to 1,94,812 crore rupees, marking a 13.9% year-on-year increase from 1,71,105 crore rupees in June 2025. According to the provisional data released by the Finance Ministry, Gross...
body: Goods and Services Tax (GST) collections in the last month witnessed a rise to 1,94,812 crore rupees, marking a 13.9% year-on-year increase from 1,71,105 crore rupees in June 2025. According to the provisional data released by the Finance Ministry, Gross domestic GST revenue stood at 1,34,774 crore rupees, up 6.5% from a year ago. GST collections from imports surged 34.6% to 60.38 crore rupees, providing a boost to the overall monthly mop-up.

title: PM Modi hails doctors serving in Venezuela under Operation Amistad
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi today hailed the efforts of doctors and medical professionals who are working in Venezuela as part of Operation Amistad. In a social media post, he said that in such a challenging time, their efforts to serve those in need are...
body: Prime Minister Narendra Modi today hailed the efforts of doctors and medical professionals who are working in Venezuela as part of Operation Amistad. In a social media post, he said that in such a challenging time, their efforts to serve those in need are shining examples of how medical professionals rise to the occasion every time society faces a challenge. India launched Operation Amistad in response to devastating earthquakes in Venezuela that caused extensive loss of life, injuries and widespread destruction. Operation Amistad is a Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Relief mission in a gesture of solidarity with the people of Venezuela.

title: National Doctors Day being celebrated across country today
summary: National Doctors Day is being celebrated today across the country to honour doctors for their dedication, compassion, and invaluable contribution to protecting and improving public health. The day is observed every year on the 1st of July to commemorate the...
body: National Doctors Day is being celebrated today across the country to honour doctors for their dedication, compassion, and invaluable contribution to protecting and improving public health. The day is observed every year on the 1st of July to commemorate the birth and death anniversary of Dr. Bidhan Chandra Roy, one of India’s most respected physicians. The theme for this year is “Behind the Mask: Who Heals the Healers?”, which highlights the importance of supporting the mental, emotional, and physical well-being of doctors who dedicate their lives to caring for others. Vice President C P Radhakrishnan has extended greetings to doctors across the country on the occasion of Doctors’ Day. In a social media post, the Vice President lauded the dedication, compassion, skill and unwavering commitment of doctors in serving humanity. Mr Radhakrishnan said the country has made remarkable strides in the healthcare sector over the years. He highlighted the world’s largest COVID-19 vaccination drive, the expansion of affordable healthcare through the Ayushman Bharat initiative, the strengthening of healthcare infrastructure, and the growing adoption of digital healthcare as key milestones in the country’s journey towards better health. The Vice President said the tireless service of doctors, from protecting lives during times of crisis to advancing medical science through innovation and research, reflects the highest ideals of duty, sacrifice and compassion. He expressed profound gratitude to the medical fraternity for its invaluable contribution to nation-building and wished doctors continued strength and success in their noble mission of healing and saving lives. Prime Minister Narendra Modi extended greetings to the Doctors of the country on the occasion of National Doctors’ Day. In a social media post, Mr Modi said that their hard work, compassion and commitment form the backbone of India’s healthcare system. He added that their tireless efforts, often in the most challenging circumstances, have earned the trust and gratitude of countless people. The Prime Minister noted that over the last decade, India has made significant strides in strengthening countries healthcare ecosystems. The number of medical colleges has more than doubled, while undergraduate and postgraduate medical seats have expanded substantially. This unprecedented growth is creating greater opportunities for aspiring doctors, building a stronger healthcare workforce and ensuring that quality medical care reaches every corner of the country. PM Modi added that as the government is working towards building a Viksit Bharat, the doctors of the nation will continue to play a pivotal role in promoting preventive healthcare, advancing medical research, embracing innovation and delivering affordable, accessible healthcare for all. Union Home Minister Amit Shah extended his greetings to all the doctors on National Doctors Day today. In a social media post, Mr Shah said their dedication and commitment to promoting good health, cure and create more awareness about a better way of life, paving the way for a stronger and healthier society. He wished that this day strengthen their commitment to serving society. On the Occasion of Doctors’ Day today, Union Health Minister Jagat Prakash Nadda has extended greetings to the medical fraternity for its unwavering dedication. In a Video message, Mr Nadda lauded doctors across the country for their exemplary professionalism and compassionate service to humanity. Describing Doctors as the cornerstone of the country’s healthcare system, he recalled their tireless work to alleviate suffering, save lives, and uphold the highest standards of patient care. He added that doctors also have played a vital role beyond hospitals and clinics by serving as advocates for health equity, preventive healthcare, public health awareness, and social justice. The Minister also emphasised the need to adopt technology to enhance diagnosis and treatment. He further expressed hope that the selfless commitment and resilience of doctors continue to inspire the nation and strengthen the collective pursuit of a healthier India. Nagaland Chief Minister Neiphiu Rio has extended warm greetings to doctors on the occasion of National Doctors’ Day today. Paying tribute to their dedication and invaluable service to society, Chief Minister Rio said doctors continue to care for patients with compassion and unwavering commitment. He acknowledged their tireless efforts, professional expertise, and selfless service, stating that they make a difference in the lives of many every day. National Doctors’ Day is being celebrated in the Country every year on July 1 to recognise the invaluable contribution of doctors to society and honour their dedication to saving lives.
